question.text: For companies with post-issue capital between Rs. 4,000 crore and Rs. 1 lakh crore, the minimum offer to public in an IPO must be at least __________ of the post-issue capital.

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** SEBI's minimum public offer thresholds are tiered based on post-issue capital: (a) at least 25% if post-issue capital is up to Rs. 1,600 crore; (b) at least Rs. 400 crore equivalent if between Rs. 1,600 crore and Rs. 4,000 crore; (c) at least 10% if between Rs. 4,000 crore and Rs. 1 lakh crore. This tiered structure accommodates very large companies where a 25% public float would be impractical.
**The Trap:** 25% is the default threshold for smaller companies and is the most commonly remembered figure. Students may incorrectly apply the 25% rule to all company sizes. The 15% and 20% options are plausible-sounding but do not correspond to any actual SEBI threshold.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Minimum public offer requirements are tested in NISM Series XII, IX (Merchant Banking), and are critical for understanding listing obligations.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember the three-tier structure: up to Rs. 1,600 crore = 25%; Rs. 1,600-4,000 crore = Rs. 400 crore worth; Rs. 4,000 crore to Rs. 1 lakh crore = 10%.
